seo.title: Building Cookie consent implementation with Plausible Ana...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Cookie consent implementation with Plausible Analytics and Fathom.

content.intro: This guide provides actionable steps to implement GDPR compliance for European SaaS infrastructure. Focuses on data mapping, consent mechanisms, privacy-first analytics, and cross-border data transfers. Designed for developers handling EU user data with practical implementation checks.
prerequisites: Inventory of all user data types processed
Access to Cookiebot/Termly for consent management
Legal review process for policy documentation

steps.title: Map data processing flows
steps.explanation: Document all user data types (identifiers, IP addresses, cookies) and processing purposes. Use tools like PostHog to visualize data movement between systems.

code.content:
Data Flow Diagram:
1. User Signup → [Email, IP] → Supabase
2. Session Tracking → [Cookie] → Cookiebot
3. Analytics → [Event Data] → Plausible

pitfalls: Missing third-party data handlers in inventory
Not distinguishing between 'explicit consent' and 'legitimate interest' data

steps.title: Implement consent management
steps.explanation: Integrate Cookiebot or Termly to manage consent for non-essential cookies. Configure separate toggles for analytics, marketing, and functional cookies.
pitfalls: Using single-checkbox consent for multiple purposes
Not logging consent timestamps in GDPR-compliant format

steps.title: Configure cookie consent banner
steps.explanation: Deploy a cookie consent banner that allows granular control. Use Cookiebot's configuration to set default states for different cookie categories.
pitfalls: Not providing an 'Accept All' option for functional cookies
Failing to refresh banner after consent changes

steps.title: Set up privacy-first analytics
steps.explanation: Replace Google Analytics with Plausible/Fathom. Configure tracking to exclude PII and use anonymized IP addresses.
pitfalls: Using analytics tools that require consent banners
Not disabling tracking for EU users in non-EU regions

steps.title: Create data deletion workflows
steps.explanation: Implement /delete-account endpoints that permanently remove user data from databases and backups. Use Supabase's row-level security to enforce deletion.

code.content:
DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 'USER_ID';
DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id = 'USER_ID';

pitfalls: Not purging data from backups or third-party systems
Leaving data in read replicas after deletion

steps.title: Handle cross-border data transfers
steps.explanation: Use EU-approved SCCs or data localization. Configure PostgreSQL to block transfers to non-EU regions using IP geolocation checks.
pitfalls: Transferring data to cloud providers without SCCs
Not auditing third-party data handlers annually

content.conclusion: Verify all steps through technical implementation checks and legal review. Regularly audit data flows, update consent mechanisms when processing changes, and maintain records of processing activities for 3 years.
